extraction.one_line_summary: TARAMAE Rein took stage and red jersey from 8-rider breakaway on Picon Blanco; MOSS Enric attacked in final 50m of peloton for GC seconds.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma deliberately ceded red jersey to breakaway rider TARAMAE, avoiding early-race domestique burden while ROGLIC remains nearby GC.
MOSS Enric attacked final 50 meters with only YATES Adam responding; ROGLIC shadowing LOPEZ's wheel neutralized sprint bonus threat.
Movistar preserved climbers until final climb rather than chasing all day, contrasting with their historically aggressive early expenditure.
CARAPAZ Richard lost over 60 seconds on Picon Blanco mountaintop finish despite Olympic form, signaling immediate GC crisis.

rider_signals.TARAMAE Rein: positive: won stage and took GC lead from breakaway; InterMarche now controls race for multiple days.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: marked LOPEZ perfectly to neutralize time bonuses; isolated but controlled situation on final climb.
rider_signals.MOSS Enric: positive: strongest GC climber in final 50m, only YATES could follow; gained crucial seconds on favorites.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: lost over 60 seconds on first summit finish despite recent Olympic gold; immediate GC hopes compromised.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: lost significant time on Picon Blanco after previous day's losses; doubts about stage-race form.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: paced entire final climb for Movistar at high tempo; unusual worker role suggests LOPEZ/MOSS prioritization.
